everlaw.name: Everlaw
everlaw.short_description: Cloud-native eDiscovery platform with AI-powered document review, priced by data volume with unlimited users.

features.description: Analyzes large document sets to surface insights with direct citations, used on datasets of 10+ million documents.
features.title: Deep Dive

features.description: Automates first-pass document review with AI-driven categorization.
features.title: Coding Suggestions

features.description: Helps synthesize evidence and brainstorm case strategy from reviewed material.

features.description: AI tools that group and prioritize documents for review.

features.description: Trial preparation tool for organizing evidence into a narrative.
features.title: Storybuilder

features.description: Provides analytics and visual insight into communication data during eDiscovery.

pros: Usage-based pricing with no per-user fees or upload limits
Single-document AI actions and the Writing Assistant included in subscriptions at no extra cost
Used by law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies
Deep Dive analysis has been used on datasets exceeding 10 million documents
cons: No public pricing; quotes require scheduling a meeting or a phone call
Batch AI actions require separately purchased credits, and unused committed credits can expire
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site

faqs.answer: Everlaw uses usage-based pricing tied to the amount of data managed, with no limits on users or uploads, offered as case-based or annual subscriptions.
faqs.question: How is Everlaw priced?

faqs.answer: No, Everlaw states there are no per-user fees and unlimited user licenses are included.
faqs.question: Does Everlaw charge per user?

faqs.answer: Single-document AI actions and the Writing Assistant are included at no extra cost; batch AI actions require separately purchased credits.
faqs.question: Are AI features included in the subscription?

faqs.answer: Law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies use Everlaw for litigation, investigations, and FOIA requests.

autumn.name: Autumn
autumn.short_description: Open-source billing platform that sits between Stripe and your app to handle plans, usage tracking, and AI credits.

features.description: One API for managing plans, usage tracking, and AI credit systems.
features.title: Unified billing API

features.description: Sits on top of Stripe to handle upgrades, downgrades, cancellations, and failed payments without manual webhook handling.
features.title: Stripe integration layer

features.description: Verifies whether a customer has access to a given feature before granting it.
features.title: Feature gating (check)

features.description: Records usage-based events for metered billing.
features.title: Usage tracking (track)

features.description: Supports both self-serve payment flows and custom enterprise deals from one source of truth.
features.title: Self-serve and enterprise billing

features.description: The full platform is open source (Apache 2.0) on GitHub and can be self-hosted.
features.title: Open-source self-hosting

pros: Fully open source under Apache 2.0, with source available on GitHub
Removes the need to build custom usage-tracking and billing logic
Purpose-built for AI startups billing on usage and AI credits
Used in production by companies including Mintlify and Firecrawl
cons: Detailed cloud pricing tiers are not fully published
Best suited to teams already built around Stripe
Newer project with a smaller ecosystem than established billing platforms

faqs.answer: Yes, Autumn is open source under the Apache 2.0 license, with its source code on GitHub.
faqs.question: Is Autumn open source?

faqs.answer: No, Autumn sits on top of Stripe as a billing logic layer, handling plans, usage, and upgrades/downgrades rather than replacing payment processing.
faqs.question: Does Autumn replace Stripe?

faqs.answer: Yes, since the project is open source, though the quickest way to start is through Autumn's hosted cloud service.
faqs.question: Can I self-host Autumn?

faqs.answer: It is used to manage subscription plans, usage-based billing, feature gating, and AI credit systems, particularly for AI startups.
faqs.question: What is Autumn used for?
